chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُقْرِضُ الدَّرَاهِمَ وَ يَأْخُذُ أَجْوَدَ مِنْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 117 – The man borrows the Dirhams and take the good ones from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/320)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/320)
text: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) كَانَ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ الثَّنِيُّ فَيُعْطِي الرَّبَاعَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Yunus Bin Yaqoub, from Abu Maryam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, when there happened to be the (debt of) a five-year old camel upon him<sup>saww</sup>, gave back the heavier one’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from Yunus ibn Ya’qub from abu Maryam who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, whenever owing something to someone would pay back heavier.’”
